# Contributing

## What to contribute

The aim is to collect a set of definitions for terms (words, phrases, taglines, etc.) relating to
NCAS (in any way e.g. its work, research, groups, people, themes, aims, etc.) . The set of
definitions was initiated at the NCAS Staff Meeting 2026 but we would love to expand it further.

If you are a member (or former member or affiliate) of NCAS, you are welcome to contribute new
definitions which abide by the rules of the Up Goer Five Challenge
(use only the 1000 most common words in English, see the [session slides](https://github.com/sadielbartholomew/sadielbartholomew/blob/master/talks-and-workshops/up_goer_five_ncas_staff_meeting_26.pdf) for context).

## How to contribute

You can make one or more suggestions via:

* opening an Issue on [the Issue Tracker here](https://github.com/NCAS-CMS/ncas-up-goer-five-definitions/issues);
* opening a [Pull Request here](https://github.com/NCAS-CMS/ncas-up-goer-five-definitions/pulls) to add definition(s) directly to the YAML file (a moderator will then re-generate the HTML file so they display on the built site);
* emailing Sadie on <sadie.bartholomew@ncas.ac.uk> (please use the email title 'Up Goer Five Definition Suggestions').

The preferred way is via the Issue Tracker however this does require a GitHub user account, and any
way above is fine. With Issues, a template has been provided to allow you to paste your term(s) and definition(s) in without need to add further text to contextualise.
